key_themes: Smartphone/handset weakness and soaring memory costs
Multiple recent analyst downgrades citing execution risks
Diversification into AI edge, automotive, and PCs
Attractive low valuation with dividend raise and $20B buyback
Near-term demand uncertainty vs long-term AI/auto potential
summary: QCOM sentiment is negative in mid-April 2026. The stock (~$136) is down substantially YTD from highs near $206 amid smartphone softness, memory price spikes crushing mid-range demand (with production cuts reported up to 30%), lost Apple modem share, and fresh downgrades from JPMorgan (to Neutral, PT $140, negative catalyst watch) and BNP Paribas.[[1]](https://robinhood.com/stocks/QCOM)[[2]](https://x.com/SamirKhazaka/status/2044824141656654145) While Qualcomm is advancing AI PCs/laptops, Snapdragon for smart glasses (Snap partnership), automotive ADAS (Bosch, Wayve investment), and raised its dividend ~3.4% to $0.92 with a new $20B buyback, near-term visibility is poor and diversification is viewed as too slow by critics. Valuation is compelling (forward P/E ~12-15x, low P/S vs AI peers), offering a potential long-term entry but current investor and analyst mood is cautious.[[3]](https://www.morningstar.com/stocks/xnas/qcom/quote)
sources: ~35 recent news articles and analyst reports (Yahoo Finance, TipRanks, Morningstar, Seeking Alpha, MarketBeat), ~25 relevant X posts from investors, former employees, and analysts (primarily Latest and semantic search results from Jan-Apr 2026)
